전도서 4:1.content: Then I returned and saw all the oppressions that are done under the sun: and behold, the tears of those who were oppressed, and they had no comforter; and on the side of their oppressors there was power; but they had no comforter.

전도서 4:2.content: Therefore I praised the dead who have been long dead more than the living who are yet alive.

전도서 4:3.content: Yes, better than them both is him who has not yet been, who has not seen the evil work that is done under the sun.

전도서 4:4.content: Then I saw all the labor and achievement that is the envy of a man’s neighbor. This also is vanity and a striving after wind.

전도서 4:5.content: The fool folds his hands together and ruins himself.

전도서 4:6.content: Better is a handful, with quietness, than two handfuls with labor and chasing after wind.

전도서 4:7.content: Then I returned and saw vanity under the sun.

전도서 4:8.content: There is one who is alone, and he has neither son nor brother. There is no end to all of his labor, neither are his eyes satisfied with wealth. “For whom then, do I labor and deprive my soul of enjoyment?” This also is vanity. Yes, it is a miserable business.

전도서 4:9.content: Two are better than one, because they have a good reward for their labor.

전도서 4:10.content: For if they fall, the one will lift up his fellow; but woe to him who is alone when he falls, and doesn’t have another to lift him up.

전도서 4:11.content: Again, if two lie together, then they have warmth; but how can one keep warm alone?

전도서 4:12.content: If a man prevails against one who is alone, two shall withstand him; and a threefold cord is not quickly broken.

전도서 4:13.content: Better is a poor and wise youth than an old and foolish king who doesn’t know how to receive admonition any more.

전도서 4:14.content: For out of prison he came out to be king; yes, even in his kingdom he was born poor.

전도서 4:15.content: I saw all the living who walk under the sun, that they were with the youth, the other, who succeeded him.

전도서 4:16.content: There was no end of all the people, even of all them over whom he was—yet those who come after shall not rejoice in him. Surely this also is vanity and a chasing after wind.
